zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Beaumont於1241年出生在英國戴文黑維爾時，他的父親Phillip II de Bello Monte21歲，母親Constance Verch Henry22歲。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1294年在英國諾福爾可多金享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard de Beaumont于1241年出生在英国戴文黑維爾时，他的父亲Phillip II de Bello Monte21岁，母亲Constance Verch Henry22岁。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1294年在英国諾福爾可多金享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Beaumont was born in 1241, in Sherwill, Devon, England, his father, Phillip II de Bello Monte, was 21 and his mother, Constance Verch Henry, was 22. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1294, in Docking, Norfolk, England, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de Beaumont est né en 1241, à Sherwill, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Phillip II de Bello Monte, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Constance Verch Henry, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1294, à Docking, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de Beaumont 1241 in Sherwill, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Phillip II de Bello Monte 21 und seine Mutter Constance Verch Henry 22. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1294 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Docking, Norfolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Beaumont è nato il nel 1241 nel Sherwill, Devon, England, il suo padre, Phillip II de Bello Monte, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Constance Verch Henry, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1294, nel Docking, Norfolk, England, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.Sherwill, Devon, England: イギリスデボンシェルウィル
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Beaumontが1241年にイギリスデボンシェルウィルで生まれた時，父Phillip II de Bello Monteは21歳，母Constance Verch Henryは22歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1294年にイギリスノーフォルクドクイングで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Beaumont(이)가 1241년 영국 데폰 셔윌에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Phillip II de Bello Monte(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance Verch Henry(은)는 22세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1294년 영국 노르폴크 독잉에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Beaumont nasceu em 1241, em Sherwill, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Phillip II de Bello Monte, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Constance Verch Henry, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1294, em Docking, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Beaumont родился в 1241 г. в местности ширвилл, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Phillip II de Bello Monte было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Constance Verch Henry, 22. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1294 г. в местности доккинг, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard de Beaumont nació en 1241, en Sherwill, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Phillip II de Bello Monte, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Constance Verch Henry, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1294, en Docking, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años.
